_verification.method: Multi-timeframe (15m/30m/1h/4h) stop/target grid sweep via the real shared engine (runFuturesBacktest, src/tools/backtest-tools.ts), reused directly (no duplicate/hand-rolled signal logic). Screen sizing 10x leverage / 50% margin/trade for the robustness pass, then every survivor re-verified at realistic sizing (5x leverage, 5% margin/trade) with a fresh split-sample check (first half of the lookback window vs second half, independently net-positive) and a >=15 trade minimum. Numbers below are the realistic-sizing, split-sample-passing results.
_verification.history: This file has been rewritten twice this session after finding numbers that no reproducible code path could have produced: (1) an earlier SMC/ICT pass where the engine silently returned 0 trades for those signal types (fixed, wired in, reverified); (2) THIS pass — a competing script (scripts/mega-sweep.ts) had overwritten this file with 'mega-sweep' results claiming up to 95% win rates and Sharpe 137.6. That script imports runFuturesBacktest but never calls it — it reimplements its own liquidity-sweep/Ichimoku/FVG signal detection from scratch (inconsistent with the validated smcBearishLiqSweep/ichimoku_bearish_breakout definitions elsewhere in the codebase), has NO split-sample or out-of-sample check, and picks the best Sharpe out of a large stop/target grid evaluated on the SAME window it reports results for — textbook selection-bias overfitting. Spot-checked its top claim (XRP 'Liq Sweep + Ichimoku 2h': 19 trades/95% WR/Sharpe 137.6) against the real engine with identical entry/risk params: real result was 13 trades/46% WR/Sharpe 18.0 (scripts/megasweep-spotcheck.ts). Discarded all mega-sweep numbers; do not resurrect scripts/mega-sweep.ts's output without rerunning through the real engine with split-sample validation.
_verification.dayTraderMultiTF: 2026-07-16: Swept 10 signal families x 3 symbols x 4 timeframes (15m/30m/1h/4h; 5m and 1d intentionally out of scope this pass) with day-trader-appropriate stop/target grids per timeframe (tighter on 15m/30m, wider on 4h). Split-sample filter applied at both the screen and realistic-sizing stages. Key finding: SOL previously had NO surviving short-side strategy (prior session's 1h-only, fixed-param pass) — the broader timeframe/param search recovered 3 working SOL shorts. Full sweep matrix: scripts/day-trader-sweep-output.json. Realistic-sizing reverification: scripts/day-trader-realistic-verify.ts.
_verification.confluenceEntryTest: 2026-07-16: Tested AND-combined entries (2 signals must fire on the same bar) vs the solo signal, on the real engine. Result: AND-confluence entries made every test WORSE (lower Sharpe, far fewer trades, no WR/PF improvement worth the sample-size cost) — e.g. XRP Liq Sweep alone: 163 trades/Sharpe 25.1 vs +ADX filter: 96 trades/Sharpe 19.3; ETH Bearish FVG alone: 363 trades/Sharpe 17.6 vs +RSI filter: 13 trades (too thin to trust). AND-narrowing entries is not a good confluence mechanism for this signal pool — the fusion tool's OR-entry + additive position-sizing confluence (already implemented, proven net positive: +$117/trade avg with a confluence add vs -$37 without) is the mechanism that actually works here. Full test: scripts/confluence-entry-test.ts.
_verification.rejectedPriorRound: 8 strategies from the previous (1h-only) verified round remain valid and are superseded here only where a better timeframe/param combo was found; ETH RSI>80 Short MR and SOL's original 3 rejected shorts are omitted (SOL now has real working shorts via bearish_fvg/bearish_liq_fvg/bearish_liq_sweep at 1h found in this pass instead).
_verification.trainForward2026: 2026-07-16 final validation: 2.5yr train (2023-07-16 to 2026-01-01) + true 2026-YTD forward holdout (Jan 1 to Jul 16), all 18 pool strategies, exact stated params, realistic sizing, PLUS 5m sub-bar exit resolution on the forward window (engine now supports subBars param — resolves which of stop/target hit first inside a native bar; native resolution assumes stop-first/pessimistic). Results: 14/18 HOLD (positive in both windows). 1 FWD_FAIL: eth-liq-sweep-short-2h ($0 PnL in 2026 — REMOVED from pool). 3 TRAIN_FAIL (net-negative over 2.5yr, profitable only in the recent regime — kept but flagged, size down): xrp-adx-di-cross-30m (-$1,931 train, DD 28.5%!), xrp-ob-retest-short-1h (-$302 train), eth-rsi-short-mr-1h (-$255 train). Note: 3yr-train PFs are systematically lower than the 1yr-window metrics stored on each entry (e.g. xrp-bearish-fvg train PF 1.29 vs 1yr 1.98) — 2023-24 was a less favorable regime; the stored 1yr metrics are the optimistic end. 5m exit resolution changed almost nothing (only sol-bearish-fvg-1h: +13% PnL) — with these stop/target widths, both levels rarely fall inside one native bar, and where they do the native assumption was already conservative. Full data: scripts/train-forward-output.json (scripts/train-forward-test.ts).
_verification.finalReportAudit: 2026-07-16, 3rd round: docs/FINAL-REPORT.md appeared mid-session claiming 15 walk-forward-validated strategies (up to 89% WR, Sharpe 94.7) sourced from scripts/mega-sweep.ts (already discredited, see above) plus scripts/walk-forward.ts — a THIRD independent hand-rolled backtest engine with its own duplicate liquidity-sweep/FVG/Ichimoku/volume-spike/SuperTrend signal generators, inconsistent with the validated ones in backtest-tools.ts, and no slippage modeling. Re-ran all 15 FINAL-REPORT claims through the real engine (runFuturesBacktest) at the exact claimed (timeframe, stop, target), using the same 3-fold OOS window definition FINAL-REPORT claims to use (scripts/final-report-verify.ts). Every single claim's trade count, WR, and Sharpe was wrong (trade counts off 5-20x — e.g. XRP 'ADX-DI Cross Short 30m' claimed 127 trades/yr, real engine found 685 on the SAME window). ALL FIVE '1d' timeframe claims failed outright (1-17 total trades over 2 years — statistically meaningless; Sharpe formula also numerically blows up on n<=2 trade samples, producing values like -151456794050981440, a known limitation of the variance estimator at tiny n, not a real number). Of the remaining 10 sub-daily claims, 3 had negative full-window PnL despite a narrow partial OOS pass (disqualified) and 7 were genuinely real and profitable once tested on the real engine with correct signal logic — but at very different numbers than claimed. Those 7 were re-verified at realistic sizing (5x/5%, scripts/final-report-realistic-verify.ts) and added below where they add genuine diversity (new timeframe or new symbol coverage) over the existing pool; entries where this round's numbers were weaker than the already-validated prior-round entry were left as-is. Warning headers added to scripts/walk-forward.ts, scripts/signal-scanner.ts, scripts/debug-scanner.ts (same anti-pattern). docs/FINAL-REPORT.md corrected in place rather than deleted.

config.note: maxHoldBars and interval are now PER-STRATEGY (see each entry's tf/maxHoldBars) — this pool spans 30m to 4h, no longer a single fixed interval.

signalTypes.bearish_liq_ob: ICT combo: Liquidity sweep + Order Block (short)
signalTypes.bearish_liq_fvg: ICT combo: Liquidity sweep + Fair Value Gap (short)
signalTypes.bearish_liq_sweep: Liquidity sweep of swing high + reversal down
signalTypes.bullish_liq_fvg: ICT combo: Liquidity sweep + Fair Value Gap (long)
signalTypes.bearish_fvg: Bearish Fair Value Gap - 3-candle gap down pattern
signalTypes.ichimoku_bearish_breakout: LuxAlgo-style: price breaks below the Ichimoku kumo cloud
signalTypes.adx_di_cross_short: ADX > threshold and +DI crosses below -DI (trend strength + direction change)
signalTypes.ichimoku_below_cloud_short: Price in sustained below-cloud state (not just the breakout moment)
signalTypes.ob_retest_short: SMC mitigation entry: bearish OB zone detected at ATR-adaptive displacement, entry on FIRST retest of proximal (zone invalidated if close through distal). value = impulse threshold x ATR.
